concepts.finding: Locality-Sensitive Hashing (LSH) uses hash functions that map similar items to the same bucket with high probability, enabling approximate NN search in sub-linear time for high-dimensional data.
concepts.implication: LSH provides theoretical guarantees for approximate similarity search and is foundation for many hashing-based retrieval methods used in large-scale search.
concepts.open_question: How does LSH compare with learned embeddings + ANN for query-document retrieval?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Learned embeddings with HNSW will outperform LSH-based retrieval by 15%+ recall@100 at equivalent latency for query-document matching.
